title: Dallas and Houston Robotaxi Fleets Reach Two Unsupervised Vehicles Each
summary: Crowdsourced Robotaxi Tracker data shows Tesla deployed two unsupervised vehicles in Dallas and two in Houston shortly after April 18 launch. Austin currently has 13 unsupervised vehicles active in the last 30 days.
impact_reason: Confirms small-scale unsupervised operations in new cities, providing incremental momentum and shared learning for Austin fleet expansion without direct Austin growth.

title: Tesla Launches Unsupervised Robotaxi Service in Dallas and Houston
summary: Tesla announced unsupervised robotaxi service rollout in Dallas and Houston on April 18, expanding its Texas footprint to three active cities with initial geofences of roughly 25 square miles each. The expansion demonstrates rapid scaling of unsupervised operations and aligns with the company's H1 2026 plan to launch across seven U.S. cities including Phoenix, Miami, Orlando, Tampa, and Las Vegas.
impact_reason: Clear positive catalyst showing geographic diversification of unsupervised robotaxi operations beyond Austin, establishing Texas as a major deployment hub and validating the scaling model for future rollouts, though absolute fleet sizes in new cities remain minimal (1-2 vehicles each).

title: Tesla Deploys Minimal Fleet in Dallas and Houston Launches
summary: Crowdsourced tracking data shows Tesla deployed only one unsupervised vehicle in each of Dallas and Houston at launch, expanding to two vehicles per city by Tuesday morning, compared to 13 active unsupervised vehicles in Austin. The Bay Area fleet remains significantly larger at over 500 vehicles.
impact_reason: While geographically significant, the extremely limited vehicle deployment (2 per city) reveals slow absolute scaling velocity and suggests Austin remains the primary unsupervised testing ground, making this a neutral operational reality check rather than a meaningful acceleration catalyst.

title: Tesla Austin Unsupervised Fleet Reaches 10 Model Ys
summary: Tesla's Austin robotaxi fleet achieved 10 unsupervised Model Y vehicles, marking the highest confirmed count of driverless vehicles in the service.
impact_reason: Mildly positive incremental progress in Austin unsupervised fleet growth, but the modest absolute number (10 vehicles) remains far below stated targets and demonstrates slow scaling velocity for the flagship market.

title: Elon Musk Announces Austin Robotaxi Fleet to Double to ~60 Vehicles
summary: Elon Musk stated the Austin Robotaxi fleet, currently at 29 supervised vehicles per community tracker, will double to about 60 next month. This falls short of the Q3 2025 goal of 500 vehicles by year-end.
impact_reason: Doubling fleet size signals positive growth trajectory in Austin but remains modest and supervised, limiting unsupervised scaling velocity.

title: Tesla Discloses 14 Crashes in Austin Robotaxi Operations
summary: In a February filing, Tesla reported 14 crashes involving its Austin robotaxis since launch.
impact_reason: Disclosed crashes introduce mild safety scrutiny that could slightly slow regulatory approvals or public acceptance, indirectly dampening unsupervised fleet scaling velocity in Austin.
